title: --memory not honored on Debian unstable
body:
/kind bug

I'm trying to use podman instead of docker on my debian unstable system, but I cannot get the `--memory` flag to work:

% podman run -t -i --rm --memory 8mb fedora cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/memory.limit_in_bytes
9223372036854771712
% docker run -t -i --rm --memory 8mb fedora cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/memory.limit_in_bytes
8388608

I have the following host kernel config:
% cat /proc/cmdline
... cgroup_enable=memory swapaccount=1 systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=0

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.0
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.9
Built:        Wed Dec 31 16:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/unstable 3.3.1+ds2-1 amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

comments.body:
you are running podman as an unprivileged user.  An unprivileged user has no access to the cgroup configuration on cgroup v1 so the memory setting is ignored.

You should either run podman as root, or even better switch to cgroup v2
IMHO podman should give a fatal error when the memory setting is explicitly specified and not supported by the underlying kernel.
